refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery, which are key contributors to operational availability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and fault-tolerant design
Automated fault detection and recovery procedures

potential_sources: Dependency on telemetry data from Vehicle and Safety Services
Coordination with Teleoperation for fault alerts
coupling_check.notes: The predictive maintenance DP logically maps to monitoring and health data collection, which can be modularized with clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Risk of incomplete fault detection coverage
Assumes integration with existing safety and teleoperation systems
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding redundancy DP if predictive maintenance alone does not meet availability targets.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for monitoring system health, processing telemetry data, and issuing safety alerts related to availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle and system health data.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts for detected faults impacting availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules for coordination and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on health monitoring specifics.
Primarily physical vehicle components, less suitable for centralized monitoring.
Focus on operator control, not system health monitoring.
Best containment for health monitoring and alerting functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing predictive maintenance and health monitoring in Safety Services reduces diffusion and improves fault detection efficiency.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module responsibilities
Dependency on accurate and timely telemetry data
Interface coordination complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module load and consider splitting monitoring and alerting if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (predictive maintenance and health monitoring) logically satisfies the refined FR by addressing downtime minimization and recovery.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion if Safety Services module becomes a bottleneck
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module shows highest containment score and lowest diffusion, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and alert logs is feasible within Safety Services module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of availability monitoring and alerting to Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Review module load on Safety Services to avoid diffusion increase.
Validate telemetry data reliability and interface robustness.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet criteria with acceptable coupling and containment. Verification and ownership are clear, and risks are manageable.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently decomposed and assigned; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Safety Services module can handle additional monitoring responsibilities.
open_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module.
